zh.lifeSummary: 當Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch"於大約1230年出生在威爾士布雷康史爾時，他的父親Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch"16歲，母親Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch13歲。他和Gwladus ferch Gruffudd至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch"于大约1230年出生在威尔士布雷康史爾时，他的父亲Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch"16岁，母亲Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch13岁。他和Gwladus ferch Gruffudd至少有1个儿子和2个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Ieuan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Iohannes. See John .

variants.name: Iefan
meanings: Later form of   Ieuan .

variants.name: Ifan
meanings: From Latin Iohannes. See John .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘ardour’; borne in the Middle Ages by various rulers in south-west Wales, including  Rhys ap Tewdur  (  d. 1093 ) and  Rhys ap Gruffudd  (  1132–97 ).

variants.name: Reece
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Welsh Rhys, in some cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the Welsh given name.

en.lifeSummary: When Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" was born about 1230, in Breconshire, Wales, his father, Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch", was 16 and his mother, Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch, was 13. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Gwladus ferch Gruffudd.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" est né environ 1230, à Breconshire, Galles, son père, Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch", avait 16 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch, avait 13 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Gwladus ferch Gruffudd.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" um 1230 in Breconshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" 16 und seine Mutter Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch 13. Er hatte mit Gwladus ferch Gruffudd mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" è nato il circa 1230 nel Breconshire, Galles, il suo padre, Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch", aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch, aveva 13 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Gwladus ferch Gruffudd.

ja.of Eglwys Iail lordship, Ystrad Yw commo: ウェールズブレコンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch"が約1230年にウェールズブレコンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch"は16歳，母Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Gochは13歳であった。彼はGwladus ferch Gruffuddとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch"(이)가 대략 1230년 웨일스 브레콘실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch"(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch(은)는 13세였다. 그는 Gwladus ferch Gruffudd(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" nasceu aproximadamente 1230, em Breconshire, Gales, seu pai, Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch", tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch, tinha 13 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com Gwladus ferch Gruffudd.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" родился около 1230 г. в местности брекуншир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch, 13. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Gwladus ferch Gruffudd.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ieuan ap Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch" nació aproximadamente 1230, en Breconshire, Gales, su papá, Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys "Gôch", tenía 16 años y su mamá, Mrs. Rhys ap Genillin ap Rhys Goch, tenía 13 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con Gwladus ferch Gruffudd.
